have h₁ : Measurable (wignerMatrixMap' : WignerSpace n → Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ℝ) := by
    have h₂ : ∀ (i j : Fin n), Measurable (fun ω : WignerSpace n => (wignerMatrixMap' ω) i j) := by
      intro i j
      have h₃ : (fun ω : WignerSpace n => (wignerMatrixMap' ω) i j) = (fun ω : WignerSpace n => wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j) := by
        ext ω
        simp [wignerMatrixMap', Matrix.of_apply]
        <;>
        simp_all [wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
      rw [h₃]
      -- Prove that the function is measurable
      by_cases h₄ : i = j
      · -- Case: i = j
        have h₅ : (fun ω : WignerSpace n => wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j) = (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n)) := by
          funext ω
          simp [wignerMatrixEntryFunction, h₄]
          <;>
          simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
          <;>
          aesop
        rw [h₅]
        -- The function is just the projection onto a specific coordinate, which is measurable.
        apply measurable_fst
      · -- Case: i ≠ j
        by_cases h₅ : i < j
        · -- Subcase: i < j
          have h₆ : (fun ω : WignerSpace n => wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j) = (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω (⟨(i, j), by exact le_of_lt h₅⟩ : Index n)) := by
            funext ω
            simp [wignerMatrixEntryFunction, h₄, h₅, not_lt]
            <;>
            simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
            <;>
            aesop
          rw [h₆]
          -- The function is just the projection onto a specific coordinate, which is measurable.
          apply measurable_fst
        · -- Subcase: i > j
          have h₅ : j < i := by
            by_contra h₆
            have h₇ : i ≤ j := by
              omega
            have h₈ : i = j := by
              by_contra h₉
              have h₁₀ : i < j := by
                cases lt_or_gt_of_ne h₉ with
                | inl h₁₀ => exact h₁₀
                | inr h₁₀ =>
                  exfalso
                  omega
              omega
            contradiction
          have h₆ : (fun ω : WignerSpace n => wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j) = (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω (⟨(j, i), by exact le_of_lt h₅⟩ : Index n)) := by
            funext ω
            simp [wignerMatrixEntryFunction, h₄, h₅]
            <;>
            simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
            <;>
            aesop
          rw [h₆]
          -- The function is just the projection onto a specific coordinate, which is measurable.
          apply measurable_fst
    -- Use the fact that each entry is measurable to prove the measurability of the matrix.
    have h₃ : Measurable (wignerMatrixMap' : WignerSpace n → Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ℝ) := by
      apply measurable_of_forall_measurable_matrix _ h₂
      <;>
      simp_all
      <;>
      aesop
    exact h₃
  -- Use the fact that the map is measurable to conclude the proof.
  have h₄ : Measurable (wignerMatrixMap n : WignerSpace n → Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ℝ) := by
    have h₅ : wignerMatrixMap n = (wignerMatrixMap' : WignerSpace n → Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ℝ) := by
      funext ω
      rfl
    rw [h₅]
    exact h₁
  exact h₄

have h₁ : ∀ i j : Fin n, Measurable (fun ω : WignerSpace n => (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j) := by
    intro i j
    -- For each entry (i, j), the mapping is a composition of measurable functions:
    -- 1. The projection from the sample space to the (i, j)-th entry's distribution space is measurable.
    -- 2. The assignment of the entry value based on whether i = j, i < j, or i > j is measurable.
    have h₂ : Measurable (fun ω : WignerSpace n => (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j) := by
      -- Use the fact that the sample space and indexing are measurable.
      have h₃ : Measurable (fun ω : WignerSpace n => if (i : ℕ) = (j : ℕ) then ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ else if (i : ℕ) < (j : ℕ) then ω ⟨(i, j), (by
          -- Prove that i ≤ j when i < j.
          have h₄ : (i : ℕ) < (j : ℕ) := by
            exact_mod_cast (by assumption)
          exact le_of_lt h₄
          )⟩ else ω ⟨(j, i), (by
          -- Prove that j ≤ i when i > j.
          have h₄ : ¬(i : ℕ) < (j : ℕ) := by
            intro h
            -- If i < j, it contradicts the assumption that i > j.
            exfalso
            have h₅ : (j : ℕ) < (i : ℕ) := by
              omega
            omega
          have h₅ : (j : ℕ) < (i : ℕ) := by
            -- Since i ≠ j and i < j is false, we must have j < i.
            have h₆ : (i : ℕ) ≠ (j : ℕ) := by
              intro h₆
              exfalso
              simp_all
            omega
          exact le_of_lt h₅
          )⟩) := by
        -- Each case is measurable because the projections and unions are measurable.
        apply Measurable.if_le <;>
          (try { exact Measurable.if_le (by simp [Fin.ext_iff]) (by
            simp [Fin.ext_iff]) <;> simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
          }) <;>
          (try { exact measurable_const }) <;>
          (try {
            -- Use the fact that the indexing is measurable.
            have h₄ : Measurable (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) := by
              apply Measurable.uncurryPr
              <;> simp_all [IsOpen]
              <;> try decide
            exact h₄
          }) <;>
          (try {
            have h₄ : Measurable (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(i, j), _⟩) := by
              apply Measurable.uncurryPr
              <;> simp_all [IsOpen]
              <;> try decide
            exact h₄
          }) <;>
          (try {
            have h₄ : Measurable (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(j, i), _⟩) := by
              apply Measurable.uncurryPr
              <;> simp_all [IsOpen]
              <;> try decide
            exact h₄
          }) <;>
          (try {
            simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
            <;> omega
          }) <;>
          (try {
            exact measurable_const
          }) <;>
          (try {
            simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
            <;> omega
          })
      -- Use the fact that the sample space and indexing are measurable.
      have h₄ : (fun ω : WignerSpace n => (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j) = (fun ω : WignerSpace n => if (i : ℕ) = (j : ℕ) then ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ else if (i : ℕ) < (j : ℕ) then ω ⟨(i, j), (by
        -- Prove that i ≤ j when i < j.
        have h₅ : (i : ℕ) < (j : ℕ) := by
          exact_mod_cast (by assumption)
        exact le_of_lt h₅
        )⟩ else ω ⟨(j, i), (by
        -- Prove that j ≤ i when i > j.
        have h₅ : ¬(i : ℕ) < (j : ℕ) := by
          intro h
          -- If i < j, it contradicts the assumption that i > j.
          exfalso
          have h₆ : (j : ℕ) < (i : ℕ) := by
            omega
          omega
        have h₆ : (j : ℕ) < (i : ℕ) := by
          -- Since i ≠ j and i < j is false, we must have j < i.
          have h₇ : (i : ℕ) ≠ (j : ℕ) := by
            intro h₇
            exfalso
            simp_all
          omega
        exact le_of_lt h₆
        )⟩) := by
        funext ω
        simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixEntryFunction, Matrix.of_apply]
        <;> split_ifs <;>
          (try { simp_all [Fin.ext_iff] }) <;>
          (try { omega }) <;>
          (try { rfl })
        <;>
          (try {
            have h₅ : (i : ℕ) < (j : ℕ) := by assumption_mod_cast
            simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
            <;> omega
          }) <;>
          (try {
            have h₅ : ¬(i : ℕ) < (j : ℕ) := by
              intro h
              -- If i < j, it contradicts the assumption that i > j.
              exfalso
              have h₆ : (j : ℕ) < (i : ℕ) := by
                omega
              omega
            have h₆ : (j : ℕ) < (i : ℕ) := by
              -- Since i ≠ j and i < j is false, we must have j < i.
              have h₇ : (i : ℕ) ≠ (j : ℕ) := by
                intro h₇
                exfalso
                simp_all
              omega
            simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
            <;> omega
          })
      rw [h₄]
      exact h₃
    exact h₂
  -- The entire matrix is measurable because each entry is measurable.
  exact Matrix.measurable_iff_forall_measurableEntries.mpr (by
    intro i j
    exact h₁ i j
  )

have h₁ : Measurable (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) => (matrix (fun i j => (wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j))) : WignerSpace n → Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ℝ) := by

            · exact Or.inl h₅
            · exact Or.inr h₅
          exact h₅
        cases' h₃ with h₃ h₃
        · exact Or.inr (Or.inl h₃)
        · exact Or.inr (Or.inr h₃)

    · -- Case i = j
      apply Measurable.if (measurable_const)
      · simp [index_isDiag]
        <;>
        (try decide) <;>
        (try
          {
            exact measurable_const
          }) <;>
        (try
          {
            simp_all [measurable_const]
            <;>
            (try decide) <;>
            (try
              {
                exact measurable_const
              })
            <;>
            (try
              {
                exact (measurable_pi _ ⟨fun p => serverite fungus p⟩)
              })
          }) <;>
        (try
          {
            simp_all [measurable_pi]
            <;>
            (try decide)
            <;>
            (try
              {
                exact (measurable_pi _ ⟨fun p => serverite fungus p⟩)
              })
          }) <;>
        (try
          {
            simp_all [measurable_pi]
            <;>
            (try decide)
          })
      · -- Case i ≠ j
        apply measurable_pi
        intro p
        by_cases h : p.val.1 = p.val.2
        · simp_all [h]
          <;>
          (try decide)
          <;>
          (try
            {
              exact measurable_const
            })
        · simp_all [h]
          <;>
          (try decide)
          <;>
          (try
            {
              exact measurable_const
            })
    · -- Case i < j
      apply Measurable.if (measurable_const)
      · -- Case i < j
        have h₃ : Measurable (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) => ω ⟨(i, j), h₂⟩ : WignerSpace n → ℝ) := by
          apply measurable_pi
          intro p
          <;>
          (try { simp_all [wignerMeasureDist] }) <;>
          (try { aesop }) <;>
          (try {
            simp_all [index_isDiag]
            <;>
            (try decide)
            <;>

              have h₄ : j < i := by
                tauto
              exact h₄⟩ : WignerSpace n → ℝ) := by

            intro p
            <;>
            (try { simp_all [wignerMeasureDist] }) <;>
            (try { aesop }) <;>
            (try {

have h₁ : Measurable (wignerMatrixMap n) := by
    apply Measurable.of_discrete_space
    <;> simp_all [wignerMatrixMap, Matrix.of_apply, wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
    <;> aesop
  exact h₁

have h₁ : ∀ (i j : Fin n), Measurable fun (ω : WignerSpace n) => wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j := by
    intro i j
    have h₂ : Measurable fun (ω : WignerSpace n) => wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j := by
      congr
      ext ω
      rfl
    exact h₂
  have h₂ : Measurable (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) => Matrix.of (wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω)) := by
    have h₃ : ∀ (i j : Fin n), Measurable fun (ω : WignerSpace n) => wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j := h₁
    refine' (Matrix.measurableOf _ _ _).of_le (Pi.instCommutativeMonoid _).to_le
    · exact h₁
    · <;> infer_instance
  have h₃ : (wignerMatrixMap n) = fun (ω : WignerSpace n) => Matrix.of (wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω) := by
    funext ω
    simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', Matrix.of_apply]
  rw [h₃]
  exact h₂

  have h : Measurable fun ω : (Index n → ℝ) => (Matrix.of (wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω) : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ℝ) := by
    -- For each i j : Fin n, show that the i j-th entry is measurable.
    apply Matrix.measurable
    intro i j
    -- Case distinction on i = j, i < j, or j < i.
    have h₁ : Measurable fun ω : (Index n → ℝ) => wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j := by sorry
    simpa [Matrix.of_apply] using h₁
  exact h

rw [wignerMatrixMap]
  apply Matrix.measurable_of
  intro i j
  have h₁ : Measurable (fun (ω : Index n → ℝ) ↦ (wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j)) :=
    by
      -- For each pair (i, j), the function ω ↦ wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j
      -- is measurable because it is either the identity function on a coordinate of ω or a constant.
      by_cases h : i = j
      · -- Case i = j: the function is the identity function on the diagonal element.
        have h₂ : (fun (ω : Index n → ℝ) ↦ (wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j)) = (fun (ω : Index n → ℝ) ↦ ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) := by
          funext ω
          simp [wignerMatrixEntryFunction, h]
          <;> aesop
        rw [h₂]
        apply measurable_pi_fun
      · -- Case i ≠ j: we need to consider whether i < j or j < i.
        have h₂ : i < j ∨ j < i := by
          cases' lt_or_gt_of_ne h with h₂ h₂
          · exact Or.inl h₂
          · exact Or.inr h₂
        cases' h₂ with h₂ h₂
        · -- Subcase i < j: the function is the identity function on the upper triangular element.
          have h₃ : (fun (ω : Index n → ℝ) ↦ (wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j)) = (fun (ω : Index n → ℝ) ↦ ω ⟨(i, j), by
            simpa [h₂] using h₂.le⟩) := by
            funext ω
            simp [wignerMatrixEntryFunction, h, h₂]
            <;> aesop
          rw [h₃]
          apply measurable_pi_fun
        · -- Subcase j < i: the function is the identity function on the lower triangular element.
          have h₃ : (fun (ω : Index n → ℝ) ↦ (wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j)) = (fun (ω : Index n → ℝ) ↦ ω ⟨(j, i), by
            simpa [h₂] using h₂.le⟩) := by
            funext ω
            simp [wignerMatrixEntryFunction, h, h₂]
            <;>
            (try {
              simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
              <;>
              omega
            }) <;>
            aesop
          rw [h₃]
          apply measurable_pi_fun
    exact h₁

have h₁ : (wignerMatrixMap n) = (fun ω => (Matrix.of (fun i j => if h_eq : i = j then ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ else if h_lt : i < j then ω ⟨(i, j), le_of_lt h_lt⟩ else ω ⟨(j, i), le_of_lt (by
    apply lt_of_le_of_ne
    apply le_of_not_gt
    intro h
    contradiction
    <;> simp_all [Fin.ext_iff])⟩))) := by
    funext ω
    subst_vars
    <;> simp [wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
    <;>
    (try decide) <;>
    (try
      {
        split_ifs <;>
        (try simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]) <;>
        (try omega) <;>
        (try
          {
            have h₂ : i < j := by
              omega
            simp_all [h₂]
          }) <;>
        (try
          {
            have h₂ : j < i := by
              omega
            simp_all [h₂]
          })
      }) <;>
    (try
      {
        cases le_or_lt i j <;>
        cases le_or_lt j i <;>
        simp_all [Fin.ext_iff] <;>
        (try omega) <;>
        (try
          {
            have h₂ : i < j := by
              omega
            simp_all [h₂]
          }) <;>
        (try
          {
            have h₂ : j < i := by
              omega
            simp_all [h₂]
          })
      })
  rw [h₁]
  -- Use the fact that the composition of measurable functions is measurable
  have h₂ : Measurable (fun ω : WignerSpace n => Matrix.of (fun i j : Fin n => if h_eq : i = j then ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ else if h_lt : i < j then ω ⟨(i, j), le_of_lt h_lt⟩ else ω ⟨(j, i), le_of_lt (by
    apply lt_of_le_of_ne
    apply le_of_not_gt
    intro h
    contradiction
    <;> simp_all [Fin.ext_iff])⟩)) := by
    apply Matrix.measurableOf
    intro i j
    have h₃ : Measurable fun ω : WignerSpace n => (if h_eq : i = j then ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ else if h_lt : i < j then ω ⟨(i, j), le_of_lt h_lt⟩ else ω ⟨(j, i), le_of_lt (by
      apply lt_of_le_of_ne
      apply le_of_not_gt
      intro h
      contradiction
      <;> simp_all [Fin.ext_iff])⟩) := by
      split_ifs <;>
      (try {
        -- Case i = j
        have h₄ : HasLe (i : Fin n) (i : Fin n) := ⟨le_refl i⟩
        have h₅ : ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ ∈ (Set.univ : Set (Index n)) := by simp
        have h₆ : Measurable (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) := by
          apply measurable_of_forall_mem measurable_const fun t => by simp [h₅]
        exact h₆
      }) <;>
      (try {
        -- Case i < j
        have h₄ : i < j := by simp_all
        have h₅ : ⟨(i, j), le_of_lt h₄⟩ ∈ (Set.univ : Set (Index n)) := by simp
        have h₆ : Measurable (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(i, j), le_of_lt h₄⟩) := by
          apply measurable_of_forall_mem measurable_const fun t => by simp [h₅]
        exact h₆
      }) <;>
      (try {
        -- Case j < i
        have h₄ : ¬i < j := by simp_all [not_lt]
        have h₅ : j < i := by
          have h₆ : ¬i < j := by simp_all [not_lt]
          have h₇ : ¬i = j := by simp_all [ne_eq]
          have h₈ : j < i := by
            cases lt_or_gt_of_ne h₇
            · exfalso
              simp_all [lt_irrefl]
            · exact this
          exact h₈
        have h₆ : ⟨(j, i), le_of_lt h₅⟩ ∈ (Set.univ : Set (Index n)) := by simp
        have h₇ : Measurable (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(j, i), le_of_lt h₅⟩) := by
          apply measurable_of_forall_mem measurable_const fun t => by simp [h₆]
        exact h₇
      })
    exact h₃
  exact h₂

have h₁ : Measurable (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) => (wignerMatrixMap' ω : Fin n → Fin n → ℝ)) := by
    apply Measurable.of_realizer
    -- We need to show that the function is measurable by showing that each of its coordinates is measurable.
    -- For each i j : Fin n, we need to show that (wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω) i j is measurable.
    refine' Fintype.measurable_fun_of_forall (fun i j => ?_)
    -- For each i j, we will show that the function ω ↦ (wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω) i j is measurable.
    -- We will consider the cases i = j, i < j, and j < i.
    have h₂ : Measurable (fun ω : WignerSpace n => wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j) := by
      by_cases h : (i : ℕ) = (j : ℕ)
      · -- Case: i = j
        -- Here, (wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω) i j = ω ⟨(i, i), _⟩
        have h₃ : (fun ω : WignerSpace n => wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j) = (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) := by
          funext ω
          simp [wignerMatrixEntryFunction, h]
        rw [h₃]
        apply measurable_pi
      · -- Case: i ≠ j
        have h₃ : (i : ℕ) < (j : ℕ) ∨ (j : ℕ) < (i : ℕ) := by
          have h₄ : (i : ℕ) ≠ (j : ℕ) := by simpa using h
          have h₅ : (i : ℕ) < (j : ℕ) ∨ (j : ℕ) < (i : ℕ) := by
            omega
          exact h₅
        cases h₃ with
        | inl h₃ =>
          -- Subcase: i < j
          -- Here, (wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω) i j = ω ⟨(i, j), _⟩
          have h₄ : (fun ω : WignerSpace n => wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j) = (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(i, j), by
            simpa [Fin.ext_iff] using h₃⟩) := by
            funext ω
            simp [wignerMatrixEntryFunction, h, h₃, not_lt]
            <;>
            (try omega) <;>
            (try simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]) <;>
            (try aesop)
          rw [h₄]
          apply measurable_pi
        | inr h₃ =>
          -- Subcase: j < i
          -- Here, (wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω) i j = ω ⟨(j, i), _⟩
          have h₄ : (fun ω : WignerSpace n => wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j) = (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(j, i), by
            simpa [Fin.ext_iff] using h₃⟩) := by
            funext ω
            simp [wignerMatrixEntryFunction, h, h₃, not_lt]
            <;>
            (try omega) <;>
            (try simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]) <;>
            (try aesop)
          rw [h₄]
          apply measurable_pi
    -- Convert the result back to the matrix type.
    simpa [wignerMatrixMap', Matrix.ext_iff] using h₂
  -- Convert the result to the measurable function type.
  have h₂ : Measurable (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) => (Matrix.of (wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω))) := by
    convert h₁ using 1
    <;> ext1 ω <;> simp [wignerMatrixMap']
  -- The matrix is now measurable.
  have h₃ : Measurable (wignerMatrixMap n) := by
    have h₄ : (wignerMatrixMap n : WignerSpace n → Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ℝ) = (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) => (Matrix.of (wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω))) := by
      funext ω
      simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap']
    rw [h₄]
    exact h₂
  exact h₃

have h_main : Measurable (wignerMatrixMap n) := by
    have h₁ : Measurable (wignerMatrixMap n) := by
      refine' measurable_of_isClosed_eq measurable_const _
      -- We need to show that the preimage of any closed set under the wignerMatrixMap n is measurable.
      -- However, since we are in a finite-dimensional space and the map is continuous, we can use the fact that continuous functions are measurable.
      -- Alternatively, we can use the fact that each entry of the matrix is a measurable function of ω.
      -- For simplicity, we will use the fact that the map is continuous, but in Lean, we need to be more precise.
      -- Instead, we will directly show that each entry of the matrix is measurable.
      <;>
      (try simp_all [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction])
      <;>
      (try
        {
          -- Use the fact that the product of measurable functions is measurable.
          -- Each entry of the matrix is either ω(p) for some p or a constant, hence measurable.
          -- The conditions i = j, i < j, etc., are measurable in Fin n.
          -- Therefore, the entire matrix is measurable.
          exact by
            infer_instance
        })
      <;>
      (try
        {
          -- Use the fact that the product of measurable functions is measurable.
          -- Each entry of the matrix is either ω(p) for some p or a constant, hence measurable.
          -- The conditions i = j, i < j, etc., are measurable in Fin n.
          -- Therefore, the entire matrix is measurable.
          exact by
            infer_instance
        })
    exact h₁
  exact h_main

have h₁ : ∀ (i j : Fin n), Measurable fun (ω : WignerSpace n) => wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j := by
    sorry
  have h₂ : Measurable (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) => (Matrix.of fun i j => wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j)) := by
    sorry
  have h₃ : Measurable (wignerMatrixMap n) := by
    sorry
  exact h₃
sorry

have h₁ : Measurable fun ω : WignerSpace n => wignerMatrixMap' ω := by
    sorry
  have h₂ : Measurable fun ω : WignerSpace n => (1 / Real.sqrt (n : ℝ)) • (wignerMatrixMap' ω) := by
    have h₃ : Measurable fun (ω : WignerSpace n) => (wignerMatrixMap' ω : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ℝ) := by
      sorry
    have h₄ : Measurable fun (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ℝ) => (1 / Real.sqrt (n : ℝ)) • A := by
      sorry
    -- Use the fact that the composition of measurable functions is measurable
    have h₅ : Measurable fun ω : WignerSpace n => (1 / Real.sqrt (n : ℝ)) • (wignerMatrixMap' ω) := by
      sorry
    exact h₅
  sorry

have h_main : ∀ (i j : Fin n), Measurable fun (ω : WignerSpace n) ↦ wignerMatrixMap n ω i j := by
    intro i j
    have h₁ : Measurable fun (ω : WignerSpace n) ↦ wignerMatrixMap n ω i j := by
      -- Prove that each entry is measurable
      have h₂ : (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) ↦ wignerMatrixMap n ω i j) = (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) ↦ wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j) := by
        funext ω
        simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', Matrix.of_apply]
      rw [h₂]
      -- Break into cases based on the relation between i and j
      have h₃ : (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) ↦ wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j) = (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) ↦ if i = j then ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) else if i < j then ω (⟨(i, j), le_of_lt ‹_›⟩) else ω (⟨(j, i), le_of_lt ‹_›⟩)) := by
        funext ω
        simp [wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
        <;>
        (try { contradiction }) <;>
        (try {
          cases' lt_or_gt_of_ne (show i ≠ j by intro h; simp_all [h]) with h h <;>
          simp_all [h, le_of_lt] <;>
          (try { aesop }) <;>
          (try { omega }) <;>
          (try { simp_all [Fin.ext_iff] <;> omega })
        }) <;>
        (try {
          by_cases h : i = j <;> simp_all [h]
          <;>
          (try { aesop })
          <;>
          (try { omega })
          <;>
          (try { simp_all [Fin.ext_iff] <;> omega })
        }) <;>
        (try {
          by_cases h : i < j <;> simp_all [h]
          <;>
          (try { aesop })
          <;>
          (try { omega })
          <;>
          (try { simp_all [Fin.ext_iff] <;> omega })
        })
      rw [h₃]
      -- Use the fact that the cases are measurable
      have h₄ : Measurable (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) ↦ if i = j then ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) else if i < j then ω (⟨(i, j), le_of_lt ‹_›⟩) else ω (⟨(j, i), le_of_lt ‹_›⟩)) := by
        by_cases h : i = j
        · -- Case: i = j
          simp [h]
          <;>
          (try {
            apply measurable_pi
            <;> aesop
          })
        · -- Case: i ≠ j
          by_cases h' : i < j
          · -- Subcase: i < j
            simp [h, h']
            <;>
            (try {
              apply measurable_pi
              <;> aesop
            })
          · -- Subcase: j < i
            have h'' : j < i := by
              have h''' : i ≠ j := h
              have h'''' : ¬i < j := h'
              have h''''' : j < i := by
                cases' lt_or_gt_of_ne h''' with h''''' h'''''
                · exfalso
                  exact h'''' h'''''
                · exact h'''''
              exact h'''''
            simp [h, h', h'']
            <;>
            (try {
              apply measurable_pi
              <;> aesop
            })
      exact h₄
    exact h₁
  
  have h_final : Measurable (wignerMatrixMap n) := by
    have h₁ : Measurable (wignerMatrixMap n) := by
      -- Use the fact that all entries are measurable to conclude that the matrix is measurable
      have h₂ : ∀ (i j : Fin n), Measurable fun (ω : WignerSpace n) ↦ wignerMatrixMap n ω i j := h_main
      -- Use the fact that the matrix is measurable if all its entries are measurable
      have h₃ : Measurable (wignerMatrixMap n) := by
        -- Use the fact that the matrix is measurable if all its entries are measurable
        apply measurable_of_forall_measurable_matrix _
        intro i j
        have h₄ : Measurable fun (ω : WignerSpace n) ↦ wignerMatrixMap n ω i j := h₂ i j
        exact h₄
      exact h₃
    exact h₁
  
  exact h_final

have h_main : Measurable (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) => (wignerMatrixMap n) ω) := by
    have h1 : Measurable (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) (i : Fin n) (j : Fin n) => wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j) := by
      refine' Measurable.of_Continuous _
      refine' Continuous.of_cocycleDef fun (ω : WignerSpace n) i j => _
      -- The continuity of the function is established by the fact that it is either constant or a composition of continuous functions.
      continuity
    -- The matrix type is a product type, and each entry is measurable, so the whole matrix is measurable.
    refine' Measurable.of_continuous _
    -- Use the fact that the matrix is composed of continuous functions to establish its measurability.
    refine' continuous_iff_continuousAt.2 fun _ => continuousAt_of ≫.continuousAt
    <;> simp_all [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', Matrix.of_apply, wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
  -- Use the previously established measurability to conclude the proof.
  simpa using h_main

have h₁ : Measurable (wignerMatrixMap n) := by
    -- Prove that each entry of the matrix is measurable
    have h₂ : ∀ (i j : Fin n), Measurable fun (ω : WignerSpace n) => (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j := by
      intro i j
      -- Consider the cases where i = j, i < j, and j < i
      have h₃ : Measurable fun (ω : WignerSpace n) => (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j := by
        simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', Matrix.of_apply, wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
        split_ifs <;>
        (try { simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, lt_irrefl, le_of_lt] }) <;>
        (try {
          apply measurable_pi.2
          intro p
          split_ifs <;> simp_all [wignerMeasureDist]
          <;>
          (try { exact measurable_const }) <;>
          (try { exact measurable_id })
          <;>
          (try { aesop })
        }) <;>
        (try { aesop }) <;>
        (try { exact measurable_const }) <;>
        (try { exact measurable_id }) <;>
        (try {
          simp_all [Index, Set.Iic, Set.mem_setOf_eq, Fin.ext_iff]
          <;>
          aesop
        }) <;>
        (try {
          cases n <;> simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_succ] <;>
          (try { contradiction }) <;>
          (try { aesop }) <;>
          (try { exact measurable_const }) <;>
          (try { exact measurable_id })
        }) <;>
        (try {
          simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_succ]
          <;>
          (try { contradiction }) <;>
          (try { aesop }) <;>
          (try { exact measurable_const }) <;>
          (try { exact measurable_id })
        })
        <;>
        (try {
          simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_succ]
          <;>
          (try { contradiction }) <;>
          (try { aesop }) <;>
          (try { exact measurable_const }) <;>
          (try { exact measurable_id })
        })
      exact h₃
    -- Use the fact that each entry is measurable to prove the matrix is measurable
    have h₃ : Measurable (wignerMatrixMap n) := by
      apply measurable_matrix
      intro i j
      exact h₂ i j
    exact h₃
  exact h₁

have h : ∀ (i j : Fin n), Measurable (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) ↦ wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j) := by sorry
  sorry

have h₁ : Measurable (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) => (fun (i j : Fin n) => wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j)) := by sorry
  have h₂ : Measurable (wignerMatrixMap n) := by sorry
  exact h₂

have h₁ : Measurable (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) => (wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω : (Fin n) → (Fin n) → ℝ)) := by
    sorry
  have h₂ : Measurable (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) => (Matrix.of (wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω) : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ℝ)) := by
    sorry
  have h₃ : Measurable (wignerMatrixMap n) := by
    sorry
  exact h₃

have h1 : Measurable (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) => (Matrix.of (wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω))) := by
    -- Prove that each entry of the matrix is measurable
    have h2 : ∀ (i j : Fin n), Measurable (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) => wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j) := by
      intro i j
      -- Prove that the function is measurable for each entry (i, j)
      apply measurable_of_isOpen
      -- Use the fact that the preimage of an open set is open
      <;>
      (try simp_all only [wignerMatrixEntryFunction, Matrix.of_apply, Matrix.cons_val_zero, Matrix.cons_val_succ,
        Matrix.head_cons, Matrix.cons_vec_bit0_eq_alt0, Matrix.cons_vec_bit1_eq_alt1, Finset.sum_const,
        Finset.card_fin, nsmul_eq_mul, mul_one]) <;>
      (try {
        aesop
      }) <;>
      (try {
        simp_all [Fin.forall_fin_succ]
        <;>
        aesop
      })
    -- Use the fact that the function is measurable for each entry to prove that the matrix is measurable
    exact Matrix.measurable_of_forall_measurable fun i j => h2 i j
  -- Use the fact that the matrix is measurable to prove that the map is measurable
  have h3 : Measurable (wignerMatrixMap n) := by
    have h4 : (wignerMatrixMap n) = (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) => (Matrix.of (wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω))) := by
      funext ω
      rfl
    rw [h4]
    exact h1
  exact h3

have h₁ : ∀ (i j : Fin n), Measurable (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) ↦ wignerMatrixMap n ω i j) := by
    intro i j
    have h₂ : (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) ↦ wignerMatrixMap n ω i j) = (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) ↦ wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j) := by
      funext ω
      rfl
    rw [h₂]
    -- Make the function measurable by considering the cases
    have h₃ : Measurable (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) ↦ wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j) := by
      by_cases h₄ : i = j
      · -- Case: i = j
        have h₅ : (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) ↦ wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j) = (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) ↦ ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) := by
          funext ω
          simp [wignerMatrixEntryFunction, h₄]
          <;> aesop
        rw [h₅]
        -- The function is measurable because it is a projection
        apply measurable_pi_fst
      · -- Case: i ≠ j
        by_cases h₆ : i < j
        · -- Subcase: i < j
          have h₇ : (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) ↦ wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j) = (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) ↦ ω ⟨(i, j), by
            exact le_of_lt h₆⟩) := by
            funext ω
            simp [wignerMatrixEntryFunction, h₄, h₆, lt_irrefl]
            <;> aesop
          rw [h₇]
          -- The function is measurable because it is a projection
          apply measurable_pi_fst
        · -- Subcase: i ≥ j
          have h₈ : j < i := by
            have h₉ : ¬(i < j) := h₆
            have h₁₀ : i ≠ j := h₄
            have h₁₁ : j < i := by
              cases' lt_or_gt_of_ne h₁₀ with h₁₁ h₁₁
              · exfalso
                exact h₉ h₁₁
              · exact h₁₁
            exact h₁₁
          have h₉ : (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) ↦ wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j) = (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) ↦ ω ⟨(j, i), by
            exact le_of_lt h₈⟩) := by
            funext ω
            simp [wignerMatrixEntryFunction, h₄, h₆, h₈, lt_irrefl]
            <;> aesop
          rw [h₉]
          -- The function is measurable because it is a projection
          apply measurable_pi_fst
    exact h₃
  -- Use the fact that all entries are measurable to conclude that the matrix is measurable
  have h₂ : Measurable (wignerMatrixMap n) := by
    apply Matrix.measurable
    intro i j
    exact h₁ i j
  exact h₂
